text.title: Django Exception Handling Rules
text.summary: Rules for specific exception handling in Django code: bare except antipatterns and correct patterns for ORM, HTTP, and validation errors.

rule.statement: Never use bare except: or except Exception:. Catch the most specific exception type available for the operation being wrapped.
rule.rationale: Bare except swallows every error including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit. Broad Exception hides bugs by silencing unexpected failures. Both make debugging impossible.

rule.statement: For Django ORM lookups, catch Model.DoesNotExist, not a generic exception. Raise a domain error (NotFoundError) immediately so the caller gets a typed exception.
rule.rationale: DoesNotExist is the only expected failure from .get(). Catching Exception here hides schema errors, connection failures, and programming mistakes.

antipattern.description: Bare except: or except Exception: without re-raising or logging with full context.
antipattern.reason: Hides all errors including bugs, KeyboardInterrupt, and SystemExit. The ruff S110 and B001 rules flag these; fix rather than suppress.
No logging, no re-raise. Failures become invisible. At minimum log with logger.error("...", exc_info=True) before pass.
